message:
fix(startup): checkpoint or remove stale WAL files, improve failure guidance

Two fixes:

1. cleanup_stale_db_artifacts now handles large stale WAL files (not just
   truncated ones). When no process holds the DB file, it tries PRAGMA
   wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) to merge the WAL into the main file. If the
   checkpoint fails (snapshot conflict, corruption), it removes the WAL
   entirely so startup can proceed with the main file.

2. Updated the final recovery failure message to list all three escalation
   paths: doctor repair, doctor reconstruct, and clear-and-reset-everything.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2070,8 +2070,10 @@ fn attempt_probe_recovery(config: &Config) -> ProbeResult {
             name: "integrity",
             problem: format!("SQLite corruption detected and automatic recovery failed: {e}"),
             fix: format!(
-                "Run `am doctor repair` to attempt manual recovery, or \
-                 `am doctor reconstruct` to rebuild the database from the Git archive. \
+                "Try these in order:\n\
+                 1. `am doctor repair --yes` — automatic repair from backups/archive\n\
+                 2. `am doctor reconstruct --yes` — rebuild the database from the Git archive\n\
+                 3. `am clear-and-reset-everything` — archive current state and start completely fresh\n\
                  The corrupt file has been quarantined at {}.corrupt-*",
                 db_path.display()
             ),
